s1.title: 1. Purpose
s1.p1: These Terms and Conditions (hereinafter "Terms") govern the use of the OpenChantier platform (hereinafter "the Service").
s1.p2: The Service is a web-based project management application designed for tradespeople and small construction businesses.

s2.title: 2. Acceptance of Terms
s2.p1: Use of the Service constitutes full acceptance of these Terms. By creating an account, the User acknowledges having read and accepted these Terms without reservation.

s3.title: 3. Registration and account
s3.p1: Access to the Service requires creating an account. The User agrees to provide accurate and up-to-date information.
s3.p2: The User is responsible for keeping their login credentials confidential. Any activity carried out from their account is deemed to have been performed by them.

s4.title: 4. Description of the Service
s4.f5: Progress sharing with clients via secure link
s4.p2: Available features depend on the subscribed plan (Free or Pro).

s5.title: 5. Plans and pricing
s5.p1: The Service is offered in two plans:
s5.free: Free plan: up to 3 active projects, unlimited photos and time tracking.
s5.pro: Pro plan (£15/month): unlimited projects, invoicing, interactive client sharing, team management and analytics.
s5.p2: Prices are inclusive of applicable taxes. The Publisher reserves the right to change prices with 30 days' notice to Users.

s6.title: 6. Personal data
s6.p1: The processing of personal data is detailed in our Privacy Policy. The User consents to the processing of their data in accordance with UK GDPR and applicable data protection legislation.

s7.title: 7. Intellectual property
s7.p1: All elements comprising the Service (text, graphics, software, trademarks, etc.) are the exclusive property of the Publisher or its partners.
s7.p2: The User retains full ownership of content they publish via the Service (photos, documents, project data).

s8.title: 8. Liability
s8.p1: The Publisher undertakes to use all reasonable means to ensure the availability and security of the Service.
s8.p2: The Publisher shall not be liable for indirect damages resulting from the use or inability to use the Service.
s8.p3: The User is solely responsible for their use of the Service and the data they enter.

s9.title: 9. Termination
s9.p1: The User may cancel their account at any time from their account settings. Cancellation of the Pro plan takes effect at the end of the current billing period.
s9.p2: The Publisher reserves the right to suspend or delete an account in case of breach of these Terms.

s10.title: 10. Applicable law
s10.p1: These Terms are governed by English law. Any dispute relating to their interpretation or performance shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English courts.

s1.title: 1. Data controller
s1.p1: The data controller for personal data collected through the OpenChantier Service is the publishing company, whose contact details appear in the Legal Notice.

s2.title: 2. Data collected
s2.p1: In the course of using the Service, we collect the following data:
s2.d1: Identification data: first name, last name, email address
s2.d2: Professional data: company name, company registration number, VAT number
s2.d3: Usage data: projects, photos, working hours, documents
s2.d4: Technical data: IP address, browser type, connection data
s2.d5: Payment data: processed exclusively by our payment provider Stripe

s3.title: 3. Purposes of processing
s3.p1: Your data is collected for the following purposes:
s3.f1: Provision and management of the Service
s3.f2: Management of your account and subscription
s3.f5: Compliance with our legal and regulatory obligations

s4.title: 4. Legal basis
s4.p1: The processing of your data is based on:
s4.b1: Performance of a contract (provision of the Service)
s4.b4: Compliance with our legal obligations (invoicing, accounting)

s5.title: 5. Retention period
s5.p1: Your data is retained for the duration of your use of the Service, then for 3 years after account deletion, unless otherwise required by law.
s5.p2: Billing data is retained for 7 years in accordance with accounting obligations.

s6.title: 6. Data recipients
s6.p1: Your data may be shared with the following providers, strictly for the purpose of delivering the Service:
s6.p2: No data is sold to third parties.

s7.title: 7. International transfers
s7.p1: Some of our providers are based in the United States. Data transfers are governed by Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission, in accordance with UK GDPR.

s8.title: 8. Your rights
s8.p1: Under UK GDPR, you have the following rights:
s8.r1: Right of access to your personal data
s8.r2: Right to rectification of inaccurate data
s8.r3: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')
s8.p2: To exercise these rights, contact us at the address in the Legal Notice. You may also lodge a complaint with the ICO (ico.org.uk).

s9.title: 9. Cookies
s9.p1: The Service uses strictly necessary cookies for operation (authentication, language preferences). No tracking or advertising cookies are used.
s9.p2: You may configure your browser to refuse cookies, but some features of the Service may be affected.

s10.title: 10. Security
s10.p1: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data: encryption in transit (HTTPS/TLS), per-user data isolation, and restricted access to production data.

s1.title: 1. Publisher
s1.p1: The OpenChantier platform is published by:
s1.director: Publication director: the legal representative of the company

s2.title: 2. Hosting
s2.hostAddress: 340 S Lemon Ave #4133, Walnut, CA 91789, United States
s2.dbHost: Database hosted by Neon (EU — Frankfurt, Germany)

s3.title: 3. Intellectual property
s3.p1: All content on the platform (text, images, graphics, logo, icons, software) is the exclusive property of OpenChantier SAS or its partners and is protected by French and international intellectual property laws.
s3.p2: Any unauthorised reproduction, representation, modification or exploitation of all or part of the platform is prohibited.

s4.title: 4. Personal data
s4.p1: The processing of personal data is governed by our Privacy Policy, accessible from the footer of the platform.
s4.p2: In accordance with UK GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify and delete your data.

s5.title: 5. Cookies
s5.p1: The platform uses only technical cookies necessary for the proper operation of the Service. No advertising or tracking cookies are placed.

s6.title: 6. Applicable law
s6.p1: This Legal Notice is governed by English law. Any dispute relating to the use of the platform shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English courts.
